title: God Creates the World
objective: Understand that God created the world and everything in it by His Word.

sections.content: The Bible begins with the story of creation. God created the heavens and the earth by His powerful Word. Everything He made was good and reflected His wisdom and order.

sections.text: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.
And God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good.

sections.heading: Creation by God's Word
sections.content: God did not struggle to create the world. He simply spoke, and creation came into existence. This shows God's power and authority over everything that exists.
I believe that God has made me and all creatures.

questions: What does Genesis teach about how the world began?
Why is it important that God created everything?
What does it mean that creation was 'very good'?

sections.content: Creator God, thank You for the world You made and for the life You have given us. Amen.

title: Humanity Created in God's Image
objective: Learn what it means that humans are created in the image of God.

sections.content: Among all creatures, human beings have a unique place in God's creation. God created humanity in His image and gave them responsibility over the earth.

sections.text: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them.
Then the Lord God formed the man of dust from the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life.

sections.heading: The Image of God
sections.content: Being made in the image of God means that humans were created to live in righteousness, knowledge, and holiness. Humanity was designed to live in perfect relationship with God.
God created human beings to know Him, love Him, and live according to His will.

questions: What does it mean that humans are created in God's image?
How are humans different from other creatures?
Why is every human life valuable?

sections.content: Lord, thank You for creating us in Your image and giving us life. Amen.

title: The Garden of Eden
objective: Understand the harmony between God and humanity before sin entered the world.

sections.content: God placed the first humans in a beautiful garden called Eden. In this place they lived in perfect peace with God and creation.

sections.text: The Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to work it and keep it.
And the Lord God commanded the man, saying, 'You may surely eat of every tree of the garden, but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat.'

sections.heading: Life with God
sections.content: Before sin, humanity lived in perfect fellowship with God. They trusted Him, obeyed Him, and enjoyed His creation.
God originally created humanity without sin and in perfect harmony with Him.

questions: What was life like in the Garden of Eden?
Why did God give Adam a command?
What does this teach about trusting God?

sections.content: Lord, help us trust Your wisdom and live according to Your will. Amen.

title: The Fall into Sin
objective: Learn how sin entered the world through Adam and Eve.

sections.content: Sin entered the world when Adam and Eve disobeyed God's command. Their choice brought suffering, death, and separation from God.

sections.text: So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food... she took of its fruit and ate.
Therefore, just as sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin.

sections.heading: The First Sin
sections.content: Adam and Eve chose to trust their own judgment instead of God's Word. This act of disobedience brought sin into the world.
Through the fall into sin, all humanity became sinful by nature.

questions: What caused the fall into sin?
How did sin change the world?
Why do all people need salvation?

sections.content: Merciful God, forgive our sins and help us trust Your Word. Amen.

title: God's Promise of a Savior
objective: Recognize God's first promise of redemption after the fall.

sections.content: Even after humanity sinned, God did not abandon His creation. Instead, He promised a Savior who would defeat evil.

sections.text: He shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel.

sections.heading: The First Gospel Promise
sections.content: Genesis 3:15 is often called the 'first Gospel.' It promises that a descendant of the woman will defeat the serpent and destroy the power of sin.
The Gospel promises forgiveness of sins through Christ.

questions: Why is Genesis 3:15 important?
Who fulfills this promise?
What does this teach about God's mercy?

sections.content: Thank You, God, for promising a Savior even after sin entered the world. Amen.

title: Cain and Abel
objective: Understand the consequences of sin through the story of Cain and Abel.

sections.content: After the fall, sin continued to affect human relationships. The story of Cain and Abel shows how jealousy and anger can lead to terrible consequences.

sections.text: Cain rose up against his brother Abel and killed him.

sections.heading: Sin Spreads
sections.content: The story of Cain and Abel shows how sin spreads through humanity and damages relationships.
The Fifth Commandment teaches us to protect life.

questions: What caused Cain's anger?
What does this story teach about sin?
How does God respond to sin?

sections.content: Lord, guard our hearts from anger and hatred. Teach us to love one another. Amen.

title: The Flood and God's Judgment
objective: Learn about God's judgment on sin and His mercy in preserving Noah.

sections.content: As humanity grew more sinful, God judged the world with a great flood but saved Noah and his family.

sections.text: The Lord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth.
Only Noah was left, and those who were with him in the ark.

sections.heading: Judgment and Mercy
sections.content: The flood shows that God takes sin seriously. Yet it also shows His mercy, because He preserved Noah and continued His plan of salvation.
God punishes sin but also shows mercy to His people.

questions: Why did God send the flood?
How did God show mercy to Noah?
What does this teach about God's justice?

sections.content: Lord, thank You for Your mercy and patience with sinners. Amen.

title: God's Covenant with Noah
objective: Understand the covenant God made with Noah after the flood.

sections.content: After the flood, God made a covenant with Noah promising never to destroy the earth again by flood.

sections.text: I have set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.

sections.heading: The Rainbow Covenant
sections.content: The rainbow reminds humanity that God keeps His promises and continues to care for His creation.
God remains faithful to His promises.

questions: What promise did God make to Noah?
Why is the rainbow important?
How does this show God's faithfulness?

sections.content: Faithful God, thank You for keeping Your promises and caring for Your creation. Amen.
